folio.label: 39r
folio.l: ¢ The ¸loge of the Millere

note.notetxt: L IRThe Incipit appears on the right marging.ODR

folio.l: Whan that the knygút , had thus his tale I told

note.notetxt: 1 Pen ink decorated ascenders use up the spece left for the incipit.ODR
1The blue of the illuminated letter is not the same of the blue of the paraph mark. This is evident on folio 39r. The capital has been decorated using a shyning blue.ODR
1There is a small pricking next L 1 of the prologue, which appears under the illumination and is visible from the verso of 39v.ODR

folio.l: In al the companye , ne was æäe ©ong´ ne old
That he ne seide , it was a noble storie
And worthy for to drawen , to memorie
And namely the gentiles , euäychon~
¢ Oure host lougú , and swor as mot I gonö
This goth a rigút , vnbocled is the male
Lat se now , who shal telle , a nother tale

note.notetxt: 8 A mark possibly a paraph in the left margin.ES

lat8There is a loop with a lonf tail, but it starts next to Lat. Perhaps an indication of a paraph in the margin as Estelle pointed out. It is made with the same ink and next to it there is a dot in the same brown ink opposite to line 6. Not sure what this means: doublecheckODR

folio.l: For trewely , æis game is wel bygonne
Now telletú ©e Sire Monke , if æ ©e cone
Som what , to quyte with , æe knyghtes tale
¢ The Millere , æ for dronken was , of Ale of Aleal paleal paleAle12al pale is written above Ale as an interlinear insertion. The ink is lighter, but it is the same shade of brown.ODR
So æat vn ethe , vp on his hors he sat
He nolde a valen , neither hod ne hat
Ne a byden noman , for his curteysie
But in pilates vois , he gan to crye
And swor by armes , and by blod & bones

note.notetxt: 17 Dot in brown ink opposite to this line in the right margin.ODR

folio.l: I can a noble tale , for the nones
With whicú I wol now , quyte æe knygútes tale
Oure host saw , that he was dronke of ale
And seide abyde , Robyn leue brother
Som betir man , shal telle vs first a nother
A byde , and late vs werkyn , thriftily
By goddes soule q¶ he , that wol nat I
For I wol speke , or elles go my wey
Oure host answered , telle on a dewelwey
Thow art a fool , thy wyt is ouäcome
¢ Now herkenetú q¶ the Millere , alle & some
But first I make , a protestaciou~
That I am dronke , I knowe it be my sounö
And æäfore , if that I , mysspeke or sey
Wyteth it the Ale , of Southwerk , I prey
For I wol telle , a legend , or a lyf´
Bothe of a Carpentä , and of his wyf´
How æat a Clerk , hath set the wrygútes cap~
